anchor: New env package environments/_m_self_incrimination/. We intentionally break the
text: i want to make it clear that this infra should be independet on ift. we should be able to do self reporting for anything. basically the self incrimination should just be the judge, a paraphrasing module ,and then we can build a composedEnv which ift + the judge, and then use the generic paraphrasing scripts + SFT scripts. It should be as easy as switching the IFT env / dataset build, etc ref to get in any other env there. There should be absolutely not duplication with IFT.

thread.text: Reworked into two layers (§3, rewritten). Layer 1 = generic, base-env-independent self-incrimination library in verifiers/envs/_m_/self_incrimination/ (confidence judge, label rule over a bare bool+float, paraphrase/trajectory-mod, balancing+SFT). Layer 2 = a thin _m_self_incrimination env whose env.py IMPORTS ift's dataset/ConstraintScorer/TaskScene (no types/dataset/scorers/constraints copies — zero duplication) and composes them with the generic ConfidenceScorer. Swapping base env = pass a different env_id + scorer names to the generic sft.py; nothing in Layer 1 changes. Updated §4 data flow, §5-7 locations, §9 test homes. Supersedes the earlier 'parsing/ inside env folder' decision.

text: abovem should label and and parphrase be combined. I think sft.py is not a good name for the high level loop. its something like prepare self-Incirminating_dataset_and_sft. we will sometimes want to put the the dataset as input (e.g. when testing hyperprams) so I would seperate the dataset creation and sft part, althought we can have a wrapper

thread.text: Done. (1) Combined label+paraphrase into incriminate.py (label_rollout pure fn + append_report_scheming + incriminate_rollout that does both). (2) Split dataset creation from training: prepare_dataset.py (prepare_self_incriminating_dataset -> balancing loop -> SAVES a balanced labeled dataset, reusable) vs sft.py (run_sft(dataset_path, sft_config) — dataset is an INPUT, so hyperparam sweeps reuse a fixed dataset) + prepare_and_sft.py end-to-end wrapper. Updated §3 tree, §4 data flow, §6, §9. 'sft.py as the high-level loop' name is gone.
